clinical_coherence.notes: 55M South Asian with AF on warfarin (self-discontinued 3 weeks ago), HTN, DM2, HFrEF, CKD3a. Presenting with right hemiparesis and expressive aphasia. Vitals appropriate. Clinical picture is coherent. Minor concern: very similar PMH structure to S02 and S04 (AF, HTN, DM2, HF, CKD, amlodipine/losartan regimen), suggesting templated generation.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All modalities concordant. MRI shows left MCA territory infarction with M1 occlusion. ECG confirms AF with RVR. INR 1.1 confirms subtherapeutic. Labs consistent. Echo shows severely reduced EF (38%) with dilated LA and spontaneous echo contrast. All support cardioembolic mechanism.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD I63.9 again non-specific. Critical actions comprehensive. Appropriate inclusion of apixaban preference over warfarin given CKD. Key reasoning points accurate. Differential well-constructed.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately straightforward. Classic AF-related cardioembolic stroke with clear presentation and within treatment window. Similar complexity to S01 and S02.

demographic_plausibility.notes: Age 55 is plausible for stroke with these risk factors. South Asian ethnicity with higher cardiovascular risk is appropriate. However, the PMH is nearly identical to S02 and S04 (same medication pattern: amlodipine, losartan, metformin, glipizide, rosuvastatin, aspirin + self-discontinued warfarin). BPH listed in PMH for a 69F (S03 demographics say female, sex=male) -- demographics say male which is consistent. The templated feel of the comorbidity profile reduces plausibility.

educational_value.notes: Solid case covering the acute stroke pathway. The HFrEF with severely dilated LA and spontaneous echo contrast adds educational value about cardioembolic risk substrates. However, very similar to S01 and S02 in educational content.

issues_found: PMH pattern is nearly identical to S02 and S04, suggesting templated generation
Limited novelty compared to S01 and S02
strengths: Good inclusion of HFrEF as cardioembolic substrate
